mainText:
Good morning. Here are your headlines for Sunday 24 May 2026.

AI models and products. Google released Gemini 3.5 Flash, combining frontier intelligence with incredible speed, outperforming Gemini 3.1 Pro while running four times faster than other frontier models.

UK personal finance. Inflation dropped to 2.8% in April 2026, but major mortgage lenders including Nationwide, HSBC, Halifax and Santander have begun cutting rates.

Israeli politics. 110 of 120 lawmakers voted Wednesday to advance a bill dissolving parliament for early elections.

UK politics. Labour lost 1,100 seats while Reform UK gained 1,400 in May council elections.

Sleep and wearables. Google launched Fitbit Air on May 7 2026 with shipping on May 26, a category-leading sleep tracker priced at 99 dollars.

B2B SaaS and go-to-market. Corgi raised 160 million dollars at 1.3 billion valuation in Series B, becoming Y Combinator's latest unicorn.

Today's question: With Fitbit rebranding to Google Health Premium at £7.99 monthly and your eight years of Fitbit data now sitting in Google's ecosystem, are you considering switching to a validation-study-proven alternative like Muse S Athena or Oura, and does that migration cost factor into your sleep optimisation experiment calculus?
fullText:
Good morning. Here is your daily brief for Sunday 24 May 2026.

🤖 AI models and products
- Claude Mythos Preview leads the provisional agentic leaderboard with a weighted score of 100.0%, followed by GPT-5.5 at 98.3% and Gemini 3.5 Flash at 97.3%.
- Antigravity SDK provides programmatic access to the same agent harness powering Google's products, optimized for Gemini models to define custom agent behaviors.
- WebMCP is a proposed open web standard allowing developers to expose structured tools to browser-based AI agents, with experimental WebMCP origin trial starting in Chrome 149.

💷 UK personal finance
- Chase Saver With Boosted Rate offers 4.50% AER for new current account customers in their first 31 days, with a 2.23% bonus for 12 months.
- Atom Bank offers 4.25% AER on its easy access Cash ISA and Trading 212 offers 3.60% AER on its Cash ISA, tracking the Bank of England base rate minus 0.15%.
- The 2026/27 tax year is the last year you can deposit your full £20,000 annual ISA allowance into a Cash ISA; from April 2027, the cap drops to £12,000 for under-65s.

🇮🇱 Israeli politics
- Netanyahu faces mounting pressure from ultra-Orthodox parties as his fractious right-wing coalition appears to face possible collapse.
- Naftali Bennett and Yair Lapid are seeking to broaden their opposition coalition by inviting Gadi Eisenkot, former IDF chief of staff and leader of the Yashar Party, to join their new party.
- Netanyahu has told Board of Peace representative Nickolay Mladenov that Israel will not withdraw from the yellow line in Gaza, and if Hamas continues to not disarm, the IDF will restart fighting to complete their mission.

🇬🇧 UK politics
- Health Secretary Wes Streeting resigned on 14 May citing lost confidence in Starmer's leadership.
- Reform UK took control of Havering, its first London local authority, alongside Essex county council and Sunderland.
- Labour lost control of Lambeth and Lewisham to the Green Party.

😴 Sleep and wearables
- Fitbit app rebrands as Google Health on 19 May 2026, with pricing at £7.99 per month or £79.99 per year in the UK, and three months of Google Health Premium included with every Fitbit Air.
- Whoop with its new algorithm performed nearly as well as the Oura Ring, especially for REM and light sleep, according to a recent validation study comparing multiple wearables against polysomnography gold standard.
- Muse S Athena is the only consumer sleep wearable combining EEG and fNIRS technology for 88-96 percent PSG-validated sleep stage accuracy with real-time sleep intervention and no subscription required.

📈 B2B SaaS and go-to-market
- Ethos, a London-based AI-powered expert talent matching network, raised 22.75 million dollars in Series A on May 6, 2026, led by Andreessen Horowitz.
- Nearly half of US-based B2B go-to-market leaders plan to increase their AI sales tool investment in 2026.
- The median seed round for US SaaS companies sits at 3.1 million dollars, with median post-money valuation hitting 24 million dollars in 2026, an all-time high.

Today's question: With Fitbit rebranding to Google Health Premium at £7.99 monthly and your eight years of Fitbit data now sitting in Google's ecosystem, are you considering switching to a validation-study-proven alternative like Muse S Athena or Oura, and does that migration cost factor into your sleep optimisation experiment calculus?
